issues: arrow shape inherently angular/sharp — limited cute/round charm
trail lighter elements lose contrast on cream light bg
1x silhouette reads but personality is lost at that scale
reasoning: Silhouette clarity is solid: the chunky pixel-art arrowhead reads instantly as a directional arrow at 4x on both dark backgrounds, and the diagonal trail reinforces motion direction with clean pixel edges. On dark bg the gold/orange palette pops well. On light (cream) bg the arrowhead holds but the lighter trail sparks fade into the background. At 1x the basic arrow shape is legible but tiny — no personality survives. Charm is the weak point: the design is competent action-VFX but the pointed, angular arrowhead doesn't deliver 'round/soft friendly toy' energy that the Echoes-of-Wisdom reference demands. It reads more 'cool/speed' than 'cute/sticker'. Passes silhouette clarity, marginal on charm.
